[ASSUMPTION] Items are presented as a linear guided-intake flow (Done / N/A / Needs follow-up) rather than inferred conditional branching, to avoid fabricating logic not stated in the source.

steps.question: Treaty issues. See IRS Publication 901, U.S. Tax Treaties instructions to Form 8833, Treaty-Based Return Position Section 7701(b), for a complete list of disclosure except 1. Residency tiebreaker 2. Dependent personal services clause 3. Treaty rates 4. S tate conformity 5. Non-qualified retirement plan 6. Requirement to file Form 8833 under Regs. Sec. 301 7. Form 8833 applicable as an attachment to Form 10 8. N ote that a “dual resident” taxpayer may elect not to as a resident alien (Field Service Advice 199935058 9. Issues that might require “competent authority” or p 10. L ook carefully at the “other income” clause for appli compensation, etc. For example, see the competent clause in the U.S./Belgium treaty.
